hypothesis: If protein sequence design and structure prediction are trained jointly in a single end-to-end discrete stochastic autoencoder with a straight-through Gumbel-Softmax sequence bottleneck and composite structural losses, then the model will achieve higher sequence recovery and produce designed sequences whose predicted structures more closely match target backbones (higher TM-score/lower RMSD) than separately trained baselines because end-to-end optimization enforces self-consistent coupling between sequence and structure representations.
